You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

A circular badge shape sits centered as the base, filled with deep navy blue (#1B3A5C), giving the emblem a stable, trustworthy foundation like a seal or coin.\Inside the circle, a soft gradient-free teal green (#5DA88E) leaf-like curve wraps around the lower half, forming a stylized sprout or growing plant stem to represent nurturing and growth.\Rising from the center of that sprout is a single stylized eighth-note, its curved flag rendered in warm golden orange (#F2A93B), the note's stem elongated upward like a young plant reaching toward light.\The note's round head is drawn slightly oversized and friendly, almost like a seed or bud, reinforcing the idea of budding talent.\A thin white ring traces just inside the circle's edge, separating the navy background from an outer thin navy border, giving the badge a clean, polished, medallion-like finish.\Small evenly spaced white dots or musical staff-line ticks run along the inner rim of the circle, evoking a simplified staff without literal lines, adding rhythm and texture to the border.

Center a circular badge outline in dark brown, thin and slightly imperfect like a hand-stamped seal, sitting on the warm cream background. Inside the circle, place a large stylized coffee bean in rich espresso brown, oval with a curved center seam, angled slightly for dynamism. Behind the bean, add two thin swirling lines in a lighter tan that rise and curl like steam or roasting smoke, suggesting heat and freshness. Along the inner top edge of the circle, arc a small row of dots in golden tan, representing scattered roasted beans for texture. At the bottom of the circle, add a simple curved banner shape in dark brown, empty but ready to ground the composition, giving it an artisanal, stamped-label feel. Keep all linework slightly rustic and hand-drawn rather than perfectly geometric, reinforcing the small-batch, craftsman quality.

A large circular badge outline forms the background, rendered as a thin double ring in dark navy and aged gold to evoke an old shop sign; inside the ring sits a subtle cream-colored disc for contrast. Centered within the badge stands a tall cylindrical barber pole body with rounded ends, painted in bold diagonal stripes of deep red and off-white spiraling upward, with a thin navy stripe woven between them for classic tricolor rhythm. Capping the top and bottom of the cylinder are two brass-gold finials shaped like small domed knobs, adding a vintage metallic accent. Flanking the pole on either side, small stylized comb and scissors silhouettes in dark charcoal rest at a slight angle, tucked near the base to signal barbershop craft without crowding the main pole. Along the inner curve of the badge ring, decorative small dots in gold are spaced evenly like vintage signage studs, reinforcing the classic emblem feel. The overall composition is symmetrical and centered, with the barber pole as the clear focal point against the warm cream background.

Centered at the bottom sits a woven basket rendered with simple crisscross lines in warm brown, its curved handle arching upward. Rising out of the basket is a cluster of fresh produce: a round orange pumpkin-like shape on the left, a bunch of green leafy carrot tops in the middle, and a plump red-orange tomato with a green stem on the right, all simplified into bold flat shapes. Above the produce, a small yellow sun with short triangular rays peeks from the top of the circle to suggest freshness and outdoor growth.
